source_of_truth: draft.md contains manuscript prose; this registry is an audit and navigation layer.

objects.description: Simultaneous impact-only observed residual model u_t = B0 epsilon_t + eta_t with unit-variance structural shocks and additive residual noise; VAR lags and dynamic responses are outside the first paper.
For the robust transformed-cumulant route, additive residual noise is independent of structural shocks and Gaussian, so every linear transform of the noise has zero cumulants above order two.
Structural shocks are normalized to unit variance, and robust-DW candidate impacts are restricted to a normalized nonsingular space to fix scale.
Population sign-restricted set built from a covariance factor P_* P_*' = B0 B0' + V and restrictions R(P_* Q) >= 0.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: If V is nonzero, the standard sign-restricted population set is based on B0 B0' + V and generally differs from the no-noise economic sign set.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: B0(D^2 - I)B0' = V, equivalently D^2 - I = B0^{-1} V B0^{-1'}, showing why positive column rescaling generally cannot absorb additive residual noise.
objects.source: vault/syntheses/Noisy residuals in recursive and sign-restricted SVARs.md

objects.description: Standard Drautzburg-Wright-style sign-admissible set built from no-noise covariance-factor candidates whose recovered shocks satisfy higher-moment independence restrictions.
objects.source: vault/papers/Refining set-identification in VARs through independence.md

objects.description: M25 working result: under residual noise, standard DW recovered shocks are not structural shocks. A rich covariance-whitened J-test inversion is generically empty in population, except for structural-coordinate rescaling cases or finite-moment aliases; finite samples can produce falsely small least-rejected regions around pseudo-candidates.
objects.proof_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs audit and early Monte Carlo triage

objects.description: Population and sample J-test inversion for standard covariance-whitened DW candidates: g_infty(Q)'Wg_infty(Q) and T ghat(Q)'What ghat(Q), with asymptotic rejection when the population criterion is bounded away from zero.
objects.verification_status: M25 working derivation complete; needs early MC and audit before draft theorem wording

objects.description: Robust DW-style sign-restricted candidate set that searches over a normalized impact space and uses mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u, rather than no-noise covariance factorization.
objects.verification_status: M24 conditional audit passed for a local normalized Gaussian-noise route; global aliases and simulation checks remain pending

objects.description: Moment-equation representation of the robust DW stack: third mixed central moments and fourth mixed cumulants of z(B)=B^{-1}u, with second moments entering only as covariance products inside fourth cumulants.
objects.verification_status: M24 algebra review passed for centered third moments and fourth cumulants with covariance-product subtractions; population verification remains pending

objects.description: Working result: under additive Gaussian residual noise, mixed higher cumulants of B^{-1}u are not shifted by noise, while second moments are contaminated; in the bivariate normalized system, local identification follows when each structural shock has a nonzero third or fourth cumulant.
objects.proof_status: conditional-local-rank-audit-passed; global uniqueness and simulation verification pending

objects.description: Planned diagnostic statement: agreement between standard DW and robust DW sets is reassuring, while divergence warns that standard DW precision may be driven by residual-noise or covariance-target misspecification.
Intuitive figure showing covariance ellipse deformation, sign-boundary movement, and standard sign-set bias under additive residual noise.
Figure showing standard DW accepted sets shrinking, emptying, or selecting least-rejected pseudo-candidates as residual noise increases.
Overlay or panel comparison of standard DW and robust DW accepted sets under no-noise and noisy DGPs.

Monte Carlo summary of true-B coverage, set width, empty-set frequency, standard-DW versus robust-DW overlap, and divergence warnings. M35 created an exploratory early screen; it is cautionary rather than final evidence because the provisional statistic is permissive under moderate noise and weak higher moments.
objects.output_source: M35 exploratory output: manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.py, manuscript/simulations/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_triage.md, manuscript/simulations/output/m35_jtest_monte_carlo_summary.json. Final manuscript table remains planned.

objects.description: M24 adversarial review of the robust DW derivation. Conditional pass: cumulant-to-moment algebra, second-moment exclusion, normalization caveat, local rank, Gaussian-noise condition, and bias language are recorded; global aliases remain for population-grid checks.

Adversarial review of simulation design and interpretation for noisy sign-set bias, standard DW false sharpening, and robust DW comparison. M30 completed the first audit layer for the M35 triage script: conditional pass for exploratory use only; population-grid and final Monte Carlo audits remain.

open_audits.question: Does the M25 standard-DW J-test derivation survive audit, especially the rich-stack generic emptying claim, structural-rescaling exception, and finite-moment alias caveats?
Do population-grid checks support robust-DW truth inclusion, reveal any remote aliases, and characterize weak-moment widening under the maintained Gaussian-noise model?

After the M30 audit of the M35 cautionary screen, do the planned population grids and Monte Carlo figures convincingly separate structural-rescaling exceptions from generic noisy covariance cases, show standard DW false sharpening or pseudo-zeros, and show robust DW widening or divergence without overstating diagnostics?
